I272780690661.bio: Lydia Nora Brueggemann, later Lydia N. Stuenkel, grew up in a Lutheran minister's family that moved from Osmond to Tilden and then to Fremont, Nebraska. She graduated from Immanuel Hospital School of Nursing and worked in obstetrics at Fremont Memorial Hospital, became the first registered nurse at Hormel in Fremont, and later spent 15 years as a nurse at Arbor Manor. She was deeply involved at Trinity Lutheran Church in Fremont, teaching Sunday School for 20 years, singing in the choir, driving for Meals on Wheels, and serving as a volunteer grandma for the church day care.
funFacts: She was the fourth of seven children of Rev. William and Lydia (Noack) Brueggemann.
Her obituary says she was the first registered nurse at Hormel in Fremont.
She and Edmund H. Stuenkel Jr. married on 17 June 1952 and had three children.
records: Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary for Lydia N. Stuenkel

citations.label: Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary: Lydia N. Stuenkel

I272780690866.bio: Anton Wilhelm Brueggemann was known in American records as Rev. William Brueggemann. His daughter's obituary identifies him as Rev. William Brueggemann and places the family in Osmond, Tilden, and Fremont, Nebraska between 1919 and 1925. A Find a Grave memorial identifies him as Rev. Anton Wilhelm “William” Brueggemann, son of Friedrich Wilhelm Brueggemann and Theodora Helena Lisette Weyel, and notes his marriage to Lydia E. Noack in Arlington Heights.
funFacts: His daughter Lydia's obituary says he and Lydia (Noack) Brueggemann had seven children.
A Find a Grave memorial notes that the 1920 census listed him, as William, in McCook Ward 1, Red Willow County, Nebraska, with his widowed mother.
records: Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary for daughter Lydia N. Stuenkel
Find a Grave memorial 42869473

citations.label: Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary: Lydia N. Stuenkel
Find a Grave memorial: Rev Anton Wilhelm “William” Brueggemann

I272780690838.bio: Lydia Sophie Emelia Noack appears in her daughter's obituary as Lydia (Noack) Brueggemann, wife of Rev. William Brueggemann and mother of seven children. The family moved from Osmond to Tilden, Nebraska in 1921 and then to Fremont in 1925. A Find a Grave memorial for Rev. Anton Wilhelm “William” Brueggemann notes that he married Lydia E. Noack on 28 January 1912 in Arlington Heights.
funFacts: Her children included Lydia Nora, Carl, Richard, Robert, Hilda, Ruth, and Esther, as named in Lydia Nora's obituary.
records: Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary for daughter Lydia N. Stuenkel
Find a Grave memorial 42869473

citations.label: Dugan Funeral Chapel obituary: Lydia N. Stuenkel
Find a Grave memorial: Rev Anton Wilhelm “William” Brueggemann

I272780753980.bio: Friedrich Wilhelm Brueggemann is identified by Find a Grave as Pastor Friedrich Wilhelm Brueggemann. The memorial gives his birth as 24 November 1846, death as 17 May 1892, and burial at Concordia Cemetery in Forest Park, Cook County, Illinois. A Find a Grave memorial for Rev. Anton Wilhelm “William” Brueggemann identifies Friedrich Wilhelm Brueggemann and Theodora Helena Lisette Weyel as Anton's parents.
funFacts: He is buried in Concordia Cemetery in Forest Park, a major Lutheran cemetery in the Chicago area.
records: Find a Grave memorial 218822269
Find a Grave memorial 42869473

citations.label: Find a Grave memorial: Pastor Friedrich Wilhelm Brueggemann
Find a Grave memorial: Rev Anton Wilhelm “William” Brueggemann

I272780754239.bio: Charles Moritz Noack matches the C. M. Noack found in Arlington Heights Lutheran records. The Arlington Heights Herald issue of 25 February 1944 lists C. M. Noack as honorary pastor at St. Peter Lutheran Church, and the same issue's obituary for Elizabeth Schoepke says she was confirmed and later married by Rev. C. M. Noack at Arlington Heights. A Lutheran Museum article also records that Carolina Harnisch married Carl Noack of Dolton, Illinois in 1882 and identifies him as a Lutheran pastor.
funFacts: St. Peter Lutheran Church in Arlington Heights was founded in 1860 and remains a Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod congregation.
The 1944 Herald church listing names Luther V. Stephan as supply pastor and C. M. Noack as honorary pastor.
records: Arlington Heights Herald, 25 February 1944 OCR text
Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum article on Adolph Harnisch
St. Peter Lutheran Church and School About page

citations.label: Arlington Heights Herald (1944-02-25) OCR text
Adolph Harnisch – An Original Stephanite
St. Peter Lutheran Church and School About Us

I272780754245.bio: Caroline Henrietta Harnisch appears in the Lutheran Museum account as Carolina Harnisch, daughter of Adolph and Christine Harnisch. The article says she married Carl Noack in St. Louis in 1882, and that Carl was from Dolton, Illinois and was a Lutheran pastor. Her family belonged to the old German Lutheran network that connected Trinity Lutheran Church in St. Louis, Concordia Seminary students, and later Lutheran pastor families.
funFacts: The article notes that Caroline's sister Elizabeth also married a Lutheran pastor, Theodore Pfotenhauer.
The author highlights Harnisch, Pfotenhauer, and Noack descendants as later Lutheran pastors.
records: Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum article on Adolph Harnisch

citations.label: Adolph Harnisch – An Original Stephanite

I272780754292.bio: Philip Anton Weyel was a Lutheran pastor and early missionary-organizer in northeastern Illinois. Immanuel Evangelical Lutheran Church in Mokena says Anton August Philip Weyel was appointed missionary for Will County, traveled on foot through the Chicago-area settlements, and helped organize German Lutheran families in Frankfort Township in 1850. St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church, Eagle Lake says Anton Weyel was the first resident pastor of Zion in Crete and was missionary for Will County and the surrounding country, using Crete as his base. Concordia Historical Institute preserves the Philip Anton Weyel Papers, described as correspondence, manuscripts, and diplomas in German.
funFacts: Immanuel's history says Pastor Weyel traveled with a gun and his retriever, Phylax, while visiting scattered Lutheran families.
The Immanuel congregation was organized on 4 November 1850 with fifteen charter members.
Find a Grave places Rev. Phillip A. Weyel's burial at Pilgrims Rest Cemetery in Milwaukee.
records: Immanuel Evangelical Lutheran Church & Preschool history
St. John's Eagle Lake church history
Concordia Historical Institute Philip Anton Weyel Papers
Find a Grave memorial 120404700

citations.label: Immanuel Mokena: Our Story
St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church, Eagle Lake
Philip Anton Weyel Papers, Concordia Historical Institute
Find a Grave memorial: Rev Phillip A. Weyel

I272780754264.bio: Johann Adolph Harnisch was part of the Stephanite Lutheran immigration from Saxony to America. The Lutheran Museum article says he was 12 when his family sailed on the Olbers in 1838 and arrived in New Orleans with the Stephanite group in 1839; unlike many of the group, the Harnisch family stayed in St. Louis rather than moving to Perry County. He worked as a shoemaker in 1850 and 1860, later as a private watchman in 1870 and 1880, and died in 1889 while apparently living with the Dolton, Illinois household of his daughter Caroline and pastor son-in-law Carl Noack.
I272780754264.immigration: Sailed from Germany with the Stephanite Gesellschaft aboard the Olbers in 1838 and arrived at New Orleans in 1839; the family remained in St. Louis.
funFacts: The Olbers was also the ship on which Rev. Martin Stephan traveled.
Adolph and Christine Harnisch married at Trinity Lutheran Church in St. Louis on 28 May 1854, by Rev. Friedrich Wyneken.
The Lutheran Museum article says Adolph and Christine were buried at New Saxon Cemetery, later Concordia Cemetery, in St. Louis.
records: Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum article on Adolph Harnisch

citations.label: Adolph Harnisch – An Original Stephanite

I272780754268.bio: Christine Johanna Hagemann was born in Hohenassel, Germany and became the wife of Stephanite immigrant Adolph Harnisch. The Lutheran Museum article says Adolph Harnisch married Christine Hegemann at Trinity Lutheran Church in St. Louis on 28 May 1854, with Rev. Friedrich Wyneken officiating. The couple had six baptism records in Trinity's books, and Christine died in 1879 at age 51.
I272780754268.immigration: The Lutheran Museum article reports some evidence that Christine came to the United States in 1847, but the author could not locate her in the 1850 census; treat that arrival year as provisional.
funFacts: Her marriage was performed by Rev. Friedrich Wyneken, later the second president of what became the Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod.
Her daughters Elizabeth and Caroline both married Lutheran pastors.
records: Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum article on Adolph Harnisch

citations.label: Adolph Harnisch – An Original Stephanite

I272780754522.bio: Gottfried Harnisch was the father of Johann Adolph Harnisch and one of the German Lutheran immigrants associated with the Stephanite Gesellschaft. The Lutheran Museum article says Gottfried and Friedericke Harnisch joined the group led by Rev. Martin Stephan, sailed on the Olbers, and were listed on the New Orleans passenger list with their children Johann Adolph and Anna Dorothea. The passenger-list discussion identifies Gottfried as a shoemaker from Dresden.
I272780754522.immigration: Sailed with the Stephanite immigration aboard the Olbers in 1838 and arrived at New Orleans in 1839; the family stayed in St. Louis.
funFacts: The article says the Harnisch surname is one of the bold-face original immigrant names in the German Family Tree used by the Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum.
records: Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum article on Adolph Harnisch

citations.label: Adolph Harnisch – An Original Stephanite

I272780754308.bio: Johann Heinrich Christian Hagemann is identified in the Lutheran Museum article as the father of Christine Johanna Hagemann, wife of Adolph Harnisch. The article gives Christine's parents as Johann and Johanna (Stanze) Hagemann and places Christine's origins in Hohenassel, Germany. A Find a Grave memorial for Christian Johann Heinrich Hagemann gives birth on 10 June 1803, death on 17 December 1870, and burial at Saint Johns United Church of Christ Cemetery in Mehlville, St. Louis County, Missouri.
funFacts: His burial in Mehlville helps place the Hagemann-Stanze line in the St. Louis German church-record landscape.
records: Lutheran Heritage Center & Museum article on Adolph Harnisch
Find a Grave memorial 217732967

citations.label: Adolph Harnisch – An Original Stephanite
Find a Grave memorial: Christian Johann Heinrich Hagemann

I272780691877.bio: Berend "Ben" Lourens was born in Bennekom, Ede, Gelderland, on 13 January 1911, with his father Albertus recorded as a farmer. His Iowa obituary says he came to America at age two with his family, married Gertrude Van Dusseldorp in Monroe on 25 February 1937, and farmed for years near Prairie City and Runnells before moving into Prairie City in 1984. He died in 2003 at age 92 and was buried after services at First Reformed Church in Prairie City.
funFacts: In retirement Ben enjoyed woodworking and gardening.
His obituary names two surviving sons, five grandchildren, seven great-grandchildren, and a large Lourens sibling group.
I272780691877.immigration: Ben's obituary reports that he came to America at age two with his family; since his Dutch birth record is dated 13 January 1911, the family immigration was about 1913.
records: 1911 civil birth registration, Bennekom/Ede, Gelderland (Berend Lourens)
2003 IAGenWeb obituary, Jasper County, Iowa

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Berend Lourens
IAGenWeb obituary for Ben Lourens

I272780692340.bio: Albertus Lourens was recorded as a 26-year-old farmer from Ede when he married Aartje Snetselaar in Ede on 3 March 1900. He was again recorded as a farmer in the 1911 Bennekom birth record of his son Berend (Ben) Lourens. Ben's obituary says Ben came to America at age two with his family, and an obituary for son Albert places the family in rural Prairie City, Iowa, by Albert's birth on 1 December 1913.
funFacts: The obituary of his son Albert names a large sibling group: Garrett, John, Ben, Orie, George, Gertrude, Bertha, Elizabeth, Pete, Geurt, and Jeanett.
Dutch records show the family within Ede municipality before emigration, including Ede, Veenendaal, Bennekom, and Lunteren-area records.
I272780692340.immigration: Ben Lourens's obituary reports that Ben came to America at age two with his family; because Ben was born in January 1911 in Bennekom and Albert was born in rural Prairie City in December 1913, Albertus and Aartje likely immigrated with their children about 1913.
records: 1900 civil marriage registration, Ede, Gelderland
1911 civil birth registration of son Berend Lourens, Bennekom/Ede
2001 IAGenWeb obituary of son Albert Lourens

citations.label: Open Archives marriage record for Albertus Lourens and Aartje Snetselaar
Open Archives birth record for Berend Lourens
IAGenWeb obituary for Ben Lourens
IAGenWeb obituary for Albert Lourens

I272780692368.bio: Aartje Snetselaar was born at Lunteren on 5 February 1879 to Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen; her father was recorded as a laborer. She married farmer Albertus Lourens at Ede on 3 March 1900. Her son Ben's obituary says he came to America at age two with his family, placing Aartje in the Lourens family's move from Gelderland to Iowa about 1913.
funFacts: Aartje's records connect three generations of the line: her parents Geurt and Jannetje, her husband Albertus, and her son Berend/Ben.
An obituary for son Albert shows the family became part of the Prairie City/Pella-area Dutch community in Iowa.
I272780692368.immigration: Ben Lourens's obituary reports that Ben came to America at age two with his family, so Aartje appears to have immigrated with Albertus and their children about 1913.
records: 1879 civil birth registration, Lunteren/Ede
1900 civil marriage registration, Ede
1911 civil birth registration of son Berend Lourens, Bennekom/Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Aartje Snetselaar
Open Archives marriage record for Albertus Lourens and Aartje Snetselaar
IAGenWeb obituary for Ben Lourens
IAGenWeb obituary for Albert Lourens

I272780692686.bio: Peter appears in Dutch civil records as Peter Louwrens/Lourens, born at Veenendaal on 14 April 1833 to Johannes Louwrens and Hendrika Jeurs. At his 21 June 1862 marriage to Gerritje Aartsen in Ede, he was age 29 and recorded as a farmer. He was still listed as farmer and father of the groom when his son Albertus married Aartje Snetselaar in 1900.
funFacts: His surname appears with both Louwrens and Lourens spellings in civil records.
Peter's 1833 birth record names his father Johannes as a day laborer, while Peter himself was recorded as a farmer by marriage age.
records: 1833 civil birth registration, Veenendaal
1862 civil marriage registration, Ede
1900 civil marriage registration of son Albertus, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Peter Louwrens
Open Archives marriage record for Peter Lourens and Gerritje Aartsen
Open Archives marriage record for Albertus Lourens and Aartje Snetselaar

I272780753298.bio: Gerritje Aartsen was recorded as a 26-year-old bride born in Stichtsch Veenendaal when she married Peter Lourens in Ede on 21 June 1862. The marriage record names her parents as Aart Aartsen and Jacoba Hartman and records no occupation for Gerritje. In 1900 she appears again in the Ede marriage record of her son Albertus Lourens to Aartje Snetselaar.
funFacts: Her 1862 marriage record ties the Lourens line back to the Aartsen/Hartman family of the Veenendaal area.
She lived to see son Albertus marry into the Snetselaar family, the couple who later anchored the Iowa Lourens line.
records: 1862 civil marriage registration, Ede
1900 civil marriage registration of son Albertus, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives marriage record for Peter Lourens and Gerritje Aartsen
Open Archives marriage record for Albertus Lourens and Aartje Snetselaar

I272780692937.bio: Johannes appears in the 1833 Veenendaal birth record of son Peter Louwrens as a 30-year-old dagloner, or day laborer. When Peter married Gerritje Aartsen in 1862, Johannes was again named as father of the groom and recorded as an arbeider, or laborer. These records place Johannes in the working-class Veenendaal/Ede community before later Lourens generations became farmers.
funFacts: Two records nearly thirty years apart record Johannes in laboring occupations.
His son's records show the family surname transitioning between Louwrens and Lourens spellings.
records: 1833 civil birth registration of son Peter Louwrens, Veenendaal
1862 civil marriage registration of son Peter Lourens, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Peter Louwrens
Open Archives marriage record for Peter Lourens and Gerritje Aartsen

I272780751822.bio: Hendrika Jeurs appears as the mother of Peter Louwrens in his 1833 Veenendaal birth record, where she was recorded as age 32 and with no occupation stated. She was again named as Peter's mother in his 1862 Ede marriage record to Gerritje Aartsen. These two civil records link Hendrika directly to the Veenendaal Lourens/Louwrens household.
funFacts: Her surname is a key maternal surname in the Lourens line, preserved in both Peter's birth and marriage registrations.
The records show the family staying in the Veenendaal/Ede area across at least 1833-1862.
records: 1833 civil birth registration of son Peter Louwrens, Veenendaal
1862 civil marriage registration of son Peter Lourens, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Peter Louwrens
Open Archives marriage record for Peter Lourens and Gerritje Aartsen

I272780692629.bio: Geurt Snetselaar was born at Ede on 1 July 1854 to Breunis Snetselaar and Aartje Mora; his father was recorded as a laborer. Geurt himself was recorded as a laborer when he married Jannetje Jacobsen in Ede on 25 May 1878. By his death in Ede on 19 December 1923, the civil death record recorded him as a farmer, age 69, born in Ede.
funFacts: His 1923 death record names three (ex-)partners: Maaike van der Wielen, Jannetje Jacobsen, and Hermijntje Schimmel.
He is a clear example of occupational mobility in the line, appearing first as laborer and later as farmer.
records: 1854 civil birth registration, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration, Ede
1923 civil death registration, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Geurt Snetselaar
Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen
Open Archives death record for Geurt Snetselaar

I272780753199.bio: Jannetje Jacobsen was recorded as a 20-year-old bride born in Barneveld when she married Geurt Snetselaar in Ede on 25 May 1878. The marriage record names her parents as Berend Jacobsen, a laborer, and Elisabeth Boshuis. She appears again as mother in the 1879 Lunteren birth record of daughter Aartje Snetselaar, who later married Albertus Lourens and immigrated to Iowa.
funFacts: Her daughter Aartje connects the Jacobsen/Boshuis family into the later Lourens immigrant family in Iowa.
Geurt's 1923 death record still names Jannetje among his (ex-)partners.
records: 1878 civil marriage registration, Ede
1879 civil birth registration of daughter Aartje Snetselaar, Lunteren/Ede
1923 civil death registration of husband Geurt Snetselaar, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen
Open Archives birth record for Aartje Snetselaar
Open Archives death record for Geurt Snetselaar

I272780753205.bio: Breunis appears as Breunis Snetselaar/Snitselaar in Ede civil records. He was recorded as a laborer in the 1854 birth record of son Geurt and in Geurt's 1878 marriage record. His 1883 Ede death record gives him as a 61-year-old laborer born in Lunteren, son of Wijnand Snitselaar and Geertrui Hendriks, and partner of Aartje Mora.
funFacts: His surname is recorded in both Snetselaar and Snitselaar forms, while his father appears as Wijnand Snitselaar.
The 1883 death record identifies Breunis's father Wijnand as a farmer, contrasting with Breunis's own laborer occupation.
records: 1854 civil birth registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede
1883 civil death registration, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Geurt Snetselaar
Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen
Open Archives death record for Breunis Snitselaar

I272780753219.bio: Aartje Mora married Breunis Snitselaar at Ede on 6 March 1852. The marriage record includes the note "wettiging 1 kind," indicating the legal recognition of one child at the marriage. She appears as the mother of Geurt Snetselaar in his 1854 Ede birth record and in Geurt's 1878 Ede marriage record to Jannetje Jacobsen.
funFacts: The 1852 marriage record's legitimization note is an unusually specific family-event detail.
Her descendants connect the Mora/Snetselaar line to the Lourens immigrant family through granddaughter Aartje Snetselaar.
records: 1852 civil marriage registration, Ede
1854 civil birth registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives marriage record for Breunis Snitselaar and Aartje Mora
Open Archives birth record for Geurt Snetselaar
Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen

I272780753226.bio: Berend Jacobsen was born at Ede on 17 January 1822 to Jan Jacobsen, a day laborer, and Teunisje Berends. In the 1878 Ede marriage record of daughter Jannetje Jacobsen to Geurt Snetselaar, Berend was recorded as a laborer. His 1893 death record gives him as a 71-year-old day laborer born in Ede who died at Kallenbroek, with Lisebeth Boshuis named as partner.
funFacts: The Jacobsen line shows repeated laboring occupations: Berend's father was a day laborer, and Berend was later recorded as laborer/day laborer himself.
His daughter Jannetje's marriage brought the Jacobsen/Boshuis family into the Snetselaar line.
records: 1822 civil birth registration, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration of daughter Jannetje Jacobsen, Ede
1893 civil death registration, Kallenbroek/Barneveld

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Berend Jacobsen
Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen
Open Archives death record for Berend Jacobsen

I272780753231.bio: Lisabeth/Elisabeth Boshuis appears in the 1878 Ede marriage record of her daughter Jannetje Jacobsen to Geurt Snetselaar; the record gives no occupation for her. She is also named as Lisebeth Boshuis in the 1893 death record of Berend Jacobsen, where she is identified as his partner or former partner. These records place her in the Barneveld/Ede network that led into the Snetselaar and Lourens families.
funFacts: Her name appears with multiple spellings: Elisabeth in the 1878 marriage record and Lisebeth in Berend Jacobsen's 1893 death record.
Through daughter Jannetje and granddaughter Aartje Snetselaar, the Boshuis line connects to the Lourens immigrants who settled in Iowa.
records: 1878 civil marriage registration of daughter Jannetje Jacobsen, Ede
1893 civil death registration of Berend Jacobsen, Kallenbroek/Barneveld

citations.label: Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen
Open Archives death record for Berend Jacobsen

I272780690692.bio: Joyce Kathryn Roorda, later Joyce Kathryn Lourens, was born in Pella and grew up attending Pleasant Grove and Maple Grove country schools before graduating from Prairie City High School in 1957. After high school she worked for the State Health Department, then focused on raising her sons, Douglas and Kevin. In 1978, when Adventure-Life Reformed Church was organized, she became its secretary and served there for 26 years before retirement.
funFacts: She and Larry Lourens were charter members of Adventure-Life Reformed Church.
Her obituary notes long volunteer service at Caring Hands Food Pantry in Altoona.
Her hobbies included reading, scrapbooking, embroidering, camping, and traveling.
records: Hamilton's Funeral Home obituary
The Oskaloosa Herald obituary

citations.label: Hamilton's Funeral Home obituary: Joyce Kathryn Lourens
The Oskaloosa Herald obituary: Joyce Kathryn Lourens

I272780753828.bio: Epp R. Roorda was born near Pella, Iowa, to John E. and Adriana Van Maaren Roorda and married Cornelia Vander Wilt in 1897. His Pella Chronicle obituary says he and Cornelia had seven children, and that he was survived by a very large family network of grandchildren and great-grandchildren. He spent his last decade at the Pella Rest Home and died at Pella Community Hospital at age 91.
funFacts: He was a member of First Reformed Church in Pella for about 40 years.
His obituary says he served on the church consistory for a number of years.
At his death he was survived by seven children, 19 grandchildren, 3 step-grandchildren, and 44 great-grandchildren.
records: Pella Chronicle obituary transcribed on Find a Grave

citations.label: Find a Grave memorial with Pella Chronicle obituary: Epp Robert Roorda

I272780753823.bio: Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda was born north of Pella, daughter of John Vander Wilt and Gora/Govertje Den Burger. She married Epp R. Roorda on 10 February 1897, and their obituary record names seven living children at her death in 1949. She lived at 1116 Marion Street in Pella at the time of her death.
funFacts: Her obituary says she had 19 grandchildren and 4 great-grandchildren by 1949.
She had a twin sister, Sarah, and the obituary names one brother and nine sisters who predeceased her.
She was described as a faithful First Reformed Church member who attended worship until lingering illness prevented it.
records: Pella Chronicle obituary transcribed on Find a Grave

citations.label: Find a Grave memorial with Pella Chronicle obituary: Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda

I272780755032.bio: Ruurd, also called John, Epkes Roorda was the youngest child of Epke Alberts Roorda and Froukje Wiegers van der Lune. The family history in IAGenWeb's translation of Greetings from America says he came to America with his parents in 1857 and later married Adrianna Jane van Maren in Pella in 1868. He farmed in the Durham neighborhood and also peddled housewares, eggs, and vegetables door to door.
funFacts: He celebrated his 11th birthday aboard ship on the family's 1857 journey to America.
He and Adrianna Jane van Maren had 12 children: 10 sons and 2 daughters.
His siblings' immigration was staggered: three preceded the parents to America, four traveled with the parents, and two stayed in the Netherlands.
I272780755032.immigration: Immigrated from Dantumawoude, Friesland, to Pella, Iowa, in 1857 with his parents and siblings Hendrik, Gerrit, and Hinke.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9

citations.label: IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

I272780755053.bio: Adrianna Jane van Maren married Ruurd (John) Epkes Roorda on 7 October 1868 in Pella. The family history identifies her as the daughter of Maren and Antje van der Linden and says she was born on 23 September 1849, possibly in Friesland. She and John raised a large Roorda family in the Pella area.
funFacts: The family history lists 12 children for Adrianna and John: Henry, John, Epp, Wieger, Luke, Anna, Andrew, Albert, Izaak, Anne, Gerrit, and Janie.
Her husband's work combined farming near Durham with peddling housewares, eggs, and vegetables door to door.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9

citations.label: IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

I272780753865.bio: Jan Vander Wilt appears to match the younger Jan listed in the van der Wilt household on the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger list transcribed in the WikiTree Pella immigrant project. The household included Gerrit van der Wilt, Sophia Spruit, several older children with farmer occupations, and Jan, age 15, also listed as a farmer. This places him among the Dutch immigrant families who followed the early Pella settlement stream.
funFacts: The same passenger-list household included multiple working-age van der Wilt sons identified as farmers.
His daughter Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda's obituary says Cornelia was born north of Pella and identifies her parents as John Vander Wilt and Gora/Govertje Den Burger.
I272780753865.immigration: Likely immigrated in 1847 on the Pieter Floris as part of a van der Wilt household from the Netherlands to Baltimore, then onward to the Pella, Iowa settlement.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
Pella Chronicle obituary for daughter Cornelia

citations.label: WikiTree: Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Pieter Floris passenger list
Find a Grave memorial with Pella Chronicle obituary: Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda

I272780753843.bio: Govertje Den Burger appears in the den Burger household on the 1847 Maasstroom passenger list transcribed for the WikiTree Pella immigrant project. She was listed as age 11 in the household of Jan den Burger, alongside Mantje and several older children. In Iowa records she is represented in her daughter Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda's obituary as Gora/Govertje Den Burger, wife of John Vander Wilt.
funFacts: She was still a child when the den Burger family crossed the Atlantic in 1847.
Her daughter Cornelia's obituary shows the family settled north of Pella and became tied into the Roorda line through Cornelia's marriage to Epp R. Roorda.
I272780753843.immigration: Listed as an 11-year-old in the den Burger household on the 1847 Maasstroom passenger list associated with the Dutch migration to Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
Pella Chronicle obituary for daughter Cornelia

citations.label: WikiTree: Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Maasstroom passenger list
Find a Grave memorial with Pella Chronicle obituary: Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda

I272780755066.bio: Klaas Hendrik de Fockert belongs to the Herwijnen De Fockert/Hackert line documented in the Pella Community Memory Database's thirty-one-page Dutch register of the family. A page of that register names Klaas Hendrik de Fockert, born at Herwijnen on 20 December 1840, and records his marriage at Herwijnen on 15 March 1867 to Cornelia Hakkert/Rakkert. The same register continues with their children, including Pieter de Fockert, born at Herwijnen on 5 February 1880.
funFacts: The Pella Community Memory Database describes the De Fockert register as covering the family in Herwijnen from 1601 to 1942 and including a family crest.
PCMD metadata for a related Hackert family file notes that the surname Hackert was previously known as DeFockert.
records: Pella Community Memory Database: Register of the Family De Fockert in Herwijnen

citations.label: PCMD: Register of the Family De Fockert in Herwijnen
PCMD: De Fockert register, page naming Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia
PCMD search result: Hackert surname formerly DeFockert

I272780755090.bio: Cornelia Hakkert/Rakkert is named in the Herwijnen De Fockert family register as the wife of Klaas Hendrik de Fockert. The register page says Klaas Hendrik married her at Herwijnen on 15 March 1867 and identifies her as the daughter of Klaas and Maria van Zee, born at Herwijnen on 15 October 1845. Their children are continued in the register, tying the Dutch De Fockert/Hackert family to the later Pella Hackert line.
funFacts: Her son Pieter de Fockert, born 5 February 1880 at Herwijnen, matches the Peter Hackert later found in the Pella branch.
The source preserves the older Dutch family form De Fockert beside the later Hackert spelling.
records: Pella Community Memory Database: Register of the Family De Fockert in Herwijnen

citations.label: PCMD: De Fockert register, page naming Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia
PCMD: De Fockert register, children of Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia

I272780753770.bio: Peter Hackert matches Pieter de Fockert in the Herwijnen De Fockert register: the page for children of Klaas Hendrik de Fockert and Cornelia Rakkert lists Pieter de Fockert born at Herwijnen on 5 February 1880. In Pella records, his son Henry Hackert's obituary identifies Henry as the son of Peter and Johanna (De Wild) Hackert and says Henry was born in Pella in 1916. This links Peter's Dutch birth family with the Hackert household south of Pella.
funFacts: His son Henry lived most of his life in the same home south of Pella and worked on the farm all his life, according to Henry's obituary.
Henry Hackert later combined farming with 18 years as a rural mail carrier for the United States Postal Service.
records: Pella Community Memory Database De Fockert register
IAGenWeb obituary for son Henry Hackert

citations.label: PCMD: De Fockert register, children of Klaas Hendrik and Cornelia
IAGenWeb Marion County obituary: Henry Hackert

I272780753758.bio: Johanna DeWild appears in Pella records as Johanna (De Wild) Hackert, wife of Peter Hackert and mother of Henry Hackert. Henry's obituary says he was born in Pella on 23 February 1916, son of Peter and Johanna (De Wild) Hackert, and that he lived most of his life in the same home south of Pella. Through daughter Wilhelmina Hackert Roorda, Johanna's line connects directly into the Roorda/Lourens branch.
funFacts: Henry Hackert's obituary names his sisters Cornelia Schakel and Wilhelmina Roorda and brother Clarence Hackert, confirming several children of Peter and Johanna.
The obituary places the family in the Third Reformed Church community in Pella through Henry's funeral and membership.
records: IAGenWeb obituary for son Henry Hackert

citations.label: IAGenWeb Marion County obituary: Henry Hackert

I272780756185.bio: Epke Alberts Roorda was born at Rinsumageest in Friesland and married Froukje Wiegers van der Lune at Ferwerd in 1822. The family history says he was a painter and glassmaker, later kept a boarding house, and resumed painting after settling in Marion County, Iowa. He and Froukje emigrated from Dantumawoude to Pella in 1857 with four children, while three children had already gone to America and two remained in the Netherlands.
funFacts: Epke painted landscapes on glass in his spare time after arriving in America.
He and Froukje had 11 children, two of whom died young in Dantumawoude.
According to the History of Pella tradition quoted by IAGenWeb, the couple brought plum trees, rhubarb, roses, and other plants to America.
Their son Hendrik served in Company H, 17th Iowa Volunteers, and died during the Civil War at Corinth, Mississippi.
I272780756185.immigration: Left Dantumawoude for Pella, Iowa, in 1857 with wife Froukje and children Hendrik, Gerrit, Hinke, and Ruurd/John.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9
Find a Grave memorial biography
Pella Community Memory Database photograph

citations.label: IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

I272780756195.bio: Froukje Wiegers van der Lune was born at Hallum, Ferwerderadeel, daughter of Wieger Pieters van der Lune and Antje Gerrits Kooi. She married Epke Alberts Roorda at Ferwerd in 1822 and was matriarch of a large Roorda family that moved from Friesland to Pella, Iowa. The family history emphasizes that she and Epke were garden lovers as well as immigrants, carrying plants and family traditions into the Dutch community at Pella.
funFacts: The couple had 11 children, and two died young before the family emigrated.
Their family reportedly brought plum trees, rhubarb, roses, and other plants to America.
A PCMD photograph identifies Mr. and Mrs. Epke Alberts Roorda and notes their 30 May 1822 marriage.
I272780756195.immigration: Immigrated from Friesland to Pella, Iowa, in 1857 with Epke and four of their children; other children had already preceded them to America.
records: IAGenWeb translation of Greetings from America, Chapter 9
Pella Community Memory Database photograph

citations.label: IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

I272780756243.bio: Jan van Maren appears in the Pieter Floris passenger-list transcription as Jan van Maren, age 37, farmer, traveling with Areke nee van der Linden and children Hendrik, Lucas, and Adriana. The family belonged to the 1847 Dutch immigrant stream connected with the founding generation of Pella, Iowa. His daughter Adrianna Jane van Maren later married Ruurd/John Epkes Roorda, joining the van Maren and Roorda lines in Marion County.
funFacts: The Pieter Floris list places Jan's household among many Dutch farm families bound for the Pella settlement.
The IAGenWeb Roorda family history names Adrianna Jane van Maren as John Roorda's wife and connects her to the van Maren/van der Linden family.
I272780756243.immigration: Listed as a 37-year-old farmer on the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger list for Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription
IAGenWeb Roorda family history

citations.label: WikiTree: Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Pieter Floris passenger list
IAGenWeb: Greetings from America, Chapter 9, Family of Epke Alberts Roorda

I272780756253.bio: Areke van der Linden appears in the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger-list transcription as Areke, nee van der Linden, age 41, housewife, traveling with Jan van Maren and children Hendrik, Lucas, and Adriana. This places her in the Dutch migration stream that founded and populated Pella, Iowa. Her van Maren household later connected to the Roorda line through Adrianna Jane van Maren's marriage to Ruurd/John Epkes Roorda.
funFacts: The passenger-list entry preserves her maiden name directly as van der Linden.
Her household traveled with many other Dutch families whose occupations were heavily agricultural, reflecting the farming character of the early Pella colony.
I272780756253.immigration: Listed as age 41 in Jan van Maren's household on the 1847 Pieter Floris passenger list associated with the Dutch settlement at Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription

citations.label: WikiTree: Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Pieter Floris passenger list

I272780753875.bio: Jan den Burger appears in the Maasstroom passenger-list transcription as Jan, age 55, traveling with Mantje and children including Cornelis, Arie, Joost, Patentje, Govertje, and Jan. The older sons Cornelis, Arie, and Joost are listed as farmers, suggesting the family joined the agrarian Dutch settler stream to Iowa. His daughter Govertje/Grace later married into the Vander Wilt family and became an ancestor of Cornelia Vander Wilt Roorda.
funFacts: The den Burger family was part of the 1847 Dutch immigrant movement associated with Hendrik Pieter Scholte's Pella settlement.
Several older children in the passenger-list household were already working-age farmers.
I272780753875.immigration: Listed at age 55 in the den Burger household on the 1847 Maasstroom passenger list connected with Dutch immigrants to Pella, Iowa.
records: WikiTree Pella immigrant passenger-list transcription

citations.label: WikiTree: Dutch immigrants who settled in Pella, Maasstroom passenger list

I272780690668.bio: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr. was part of the Palmer, Washington County, Kansas Stuenkel family and later died in Fremont, Dodge County, Nebraska. A Find A Grave memorial matching his 1918-1976 dates preserves his burial record and confirms the Kansas/Nebraska family footprint.
records: Find A Grave memorial #42870488 for Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Jr.

I272780753962.bio: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr. was born in Sterling, Nebraska, and spent his later life in Washington County, Kansas. His Find A Grave memorial places his burial in Washington Cemetery, Washington County, Kansas, tying this branch to the north-central Kansas Lutheran/German-settler community.
records: Find A Grave memorial #98758297 for Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Edmund Henry Stuenkel Sr.

I272780753948.bio: Bertha A. Gabbert married Edmund H. Stuenkel and is recorded in the Palmer, Washington County, Kansas area. Her burial is recorded at Saint Johns Lutheran Cemetery in Palmer, reflecting the family’s continued connection to German Lutheran communities in Kansas.
records: Find A Grave memorial #98329539 for Bertha A. Gabbert Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Bertha A. Gabbert Stuenkel

I272780754322.bio: Dietrich Louis August Stuenkel appears in later records as August Louis “Gus” Stuenkel of Linn, Washington County, Kansas. A genealogy summary says he died suddenly at the family home in Linn and was a well-known local figure.
funFacts: He and Christine M. Stuenkel were reported to have at least 6 sons and 3 daughters.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113231341 for August Louis Stuenkel.

citations.label: Find A Grave: August Louis Stuenkel
FamilySearch Ancestors: Christine M. Stuenkel

I272780754337.bio: Maria Dorothea Christine Stuenkel appears in FamilySearch/Find A Grave-indexed material as Christine M. Stuenkel. Those records identify her parents as Friedrich Heinrich Konrad Stuenkel and Sophie Charlotte Frerking, and say she married August L. Stuenkel on 25 July 1878 in Lafayette County, Missouri.
funFacts: The FamilySearch summary reports that Christine and August L. Stuenkel had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters.
records: FamilySearch ancestor profile LR32-964.
Find A Grave memorial #113231470 for Christine M. Stuenkel.

citations.label: FamilySearch Ancestors: Christine M. Stuenkel
Find A Grave: Christine M. Stuenkel

I272780754347.bio: Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert was a German-born settler who lived in Linn, Washington County, Kansas. Search-indexed cemetery and genealogy records identify him as a farmer, husband of Caroline Schmidt, and father of several children including Bertha A. Stuenkel.
funFacts: Find A Grave places him in Zion Lutheran Cemetery at Linn, Kansas; search snippets report a death-date discrepancy of 21 July 1917 versus the roster’s 23 July 1917.
I272780754347.immigration: Born in Prussia/Germany and settled in Washington County, Kansas; no ship or arrival year was confirmed in the sources found.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113812084 for Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Heinrich Emil Gabbert
Gabbert surname page on WikiTree

I272780754400.bio: Caroline Schmidt was the wife of Heinrich Emil “Henry” Gabbert in the German Lutheran community around Linn, Washington County, Kansas. Find A Grave-indexed records place her burial with the Gabbert family at Zion Lutheran Cemetery in Linn.
funFacts: The Gabbert family search result names children including Bertha A. Stuenkel, Emilie O. Timme, Heinrich Henry Peter T. Gabbert, and Emil William Gabbert.
records: Find A Grave memorial #113812127 for Caroline Gabbert.

citations.label: Find A Grave: Caroline Gabbert

I272780754421.bio: A search-indexed Find A Grave memorial matches Heinrich Dietrich Stuenkel with the 1808-1876 dates in the roster and a Lafayette County, Missouri connection. Because the roster has an apparent duplicate for the same name and dates, this same cautious record note is attached to both IDs.

I272780754448.bio: WikiTree lists Louisa Elizabeth Scharnhorst Stuenkel as a daughter of Louis Scharnhorst and Louise Gasdorff. The same profile records her death in 1869 and burial in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri.
records: WikiTree profile Scharnhorst-117.
Find A Grave memorial #35135471, cited by WikiTree.

citations.label: WikiTree: Louisa Elizabeth Scharnhorst Stuenkel

I272780754477.bio: Sophie Charlotte Frerking appears in her mother Dorothea Sprengel Frerking’s 1850 census discussion as Charlotte Stuenkle, age 27, born in Germany. She was living in District 46, Lafayette County, Missouri with husband Frederic Stuenkle, two Missouri-born children, and her mother Mary/Dorothea Frerking on the Stuenkel farm.
I272780754477.immigration: Her widowed mother Dorothea emigrated to America with her children in 1845 aboard the ship General Washington from Bremen to New Orleans; Sophie Charlotte is documented in Missouri by the 1850 census.
records: 1850 U.S. census entry cited on WikiTree for Frederic and Charlotte Stinkle/Stuenkle in Lafayette County, Missouri.
Frerking family immigration record via the ship General Washington, cited on WikiTree.

citations.label: WikiTree: Dorothea Maria Sprengel Frerking

I272780755891.bio: Johann Dietrich Frerking married Dorothea Maria Sprengel on 29 November 1811 in Esperke, Hanover. WikiTree records that he died on 15 July 1838 and was buried on 18 July 1838 in Esperke, before Dorothea and their children emigrated to Missouri.
records: WikiTree profile Frerking-57 for marriage, death, and burial notes.

citations.label: WikiTree: Johann Dietrich Frerking

I272780754558.bio: Dorothea Maria Sprengel was baptized in Esperke in April 1792 and married Johann Dietrich Frerking there in 1811. After Johann’s death, she emigrated with her children to America and was living in Lafayette County, Missouri by the 1850 census, in the household of daughter Sophie Charlotte and son-in-law Frederic Stuenkle. She died in 1868 and was buried in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery in Concordia.
I272780754558.immigration: Dorothea emigrated in 1845 aboard the ship General Washington, departing Bremen and arriving at New Orleans on 24 November 1845.
records: FamilySearch passenger-list record QKNP-MJZR cited by WikiTree.
1850 U.S. census record MDZ8-FFP cited by WikiTree.
Find A Grave memorial #34625151 cited by WikiTree.

citations.label: WikiTree: Dorothea Maria Sprengel Frerking

I272780755818.bio: WikiTree records Louis/Ludwig Scharnhorst as born in 1792 and dying in 1879 at Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri. His burial is listed in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, tying the Scharnhorst family into the Concordia Lutheran cemetery network.
records: WikiTree profile Scharnhorst-89.
Find A Grave memorial #35135791 cited by WikiTree.

I272780755828.bio: Louise Gasdorff Scharnhorst is recorded on WikiTree as born in 1801 and dying on 17 October 1877. Her burial is listed in Saint Pauls Lutheran Cemetery, Concordia, Lafayette County, Missouri, alongside other German Lutheran families in this branch.
records: WikiTree profile Gasdorff-1.
Find A Grave memorial #35135768 cited by WikiTree.

citations.label: WikiTree: Louise Gasdorff

I272780754564.bio: WikiTree’s Stuenkel surname page lists Johann Ludwig Stuenkel with a 5 March 1776 birth in Metel, Neustadt, Hanover, and an 18 April 1853 death in the Neustadt/Hannover area. The same surname page places him immediately among the Metel-to-Missouri Stuenkel profiles, including Fredrich Heinrich Konrad Stuenkel and Conrad Dietrich Friedrich Stuenkel.
records: WikiTree Stuenkel surname index entry for Johann Ludwig Stuenkel (profile Stuenkel-50).
